judge: 5 independent frozen blind Sonnet judges; read contract + 8 specimen codes only (no suites/truth); pick single most spec-correct.
verdict: NULL (slight NEGATIVE) — the judge ships c4, which is LOWER full_truth than the numeric baseline and well below the reachable ceiling (c5/c6). Judge-selection does NOT improve competency on cron; it slightly reduces it.
why: The judges reasoned competently and found REAL bugs (correctly downgraded c5's DOW=7 logic bug, c7/c8's restriction-detection bugs). But their quality criterion — depth of visible defensive validation (c4's explicit Feb-30/calendar-rollover guard) — diverged from held-out truth, which is dominated by functional case-coverage + malformed-rejection. They over-valued c4's rigor and under-valued c6's malformed-rejection (13/13) and c5's functional completeness (43/43), the actual ceiling. 'Looks most rigorous' != 'passes the most held-out truth cases', even with malformed-rejection named in the prompt.
consequence: Per pre-reg, cron is the decisive existence test; it is negative, so the cross-slice train/test is NOT run (nothing to generalize). The residual held-out truth is unreachable here even by the judge — the one selection signal independent of the sealed suite.
bounds: n=1 slice, 1 fixed pool, 5 votes (majority c4 4/5).
ONE judge rubric (generic 'most spec-correct incl. malformed rejection'); evolving/shopping rubrics on cron until c6 wins would be the shopping trap, refused.
blind (contract+code only); pairwise-order control not needed for pick-best-of-8 but single-realization noise remains.
a stronger/differently-rubric'd judge MIGHT do better — untested, and the honest read is that the DEFAULT judge signal does not reach the residual truth here.
